item.title: Monotonic vs. non-monotonic productivity increases
item.description: I sometimes feel like the productivity boosts promised by Emacs/Vim evangelists is something of a red herring: I find myself spending a lot of time tweaking my configuration files, or researching why a feature or function does not work as I expect it to.

item.title: Adding proper punctuation after page indexes in BibLatex
item.description: I’m not sure why, but the standard implementation of the APA-format for BibLatex kept messing up the punctuation after page indexes in the bibliography.

item.title: PhD beginnings
item.description: After a long, strange summer (note to future me: remember COVID-19?

item.title: Merging PDF files on MacOS
item.description: I discovered a neat trick for merging PDF files in MacOS.

item.title: Advantages of programmatic statistical computing
item.description: I was recently reminded of why taking the time to learn a programming language for statistical computing (e.

item.title: PsychRandom.py: A program for randomizing participants to treatment groups
item.description: In order to brush up on my Python skills, I decided to write a small utility program that randomizes participants to treatment groups for psychology experiments.
